candidates.why: Direct total revenue label from income statement; valid top-line measure.
Not revenue; operating expense line.
Direct sales/top-line label and likely the primary operating revenue line.
Explicitly non-revenue, so not appropriate for REVT.

variables.notes: Both rows represent the same top-line across years; ordered with the more specific operating sales label first.

candidates.why: Best match for direct production/service costs on this income statement.

variables.notes: Selected the only clear direct cost line.

candidates.why: Core operating overhead/personnel cost and a common SG&A component.
Broad operating overhead bucket; suitable when no explicit SG&A line exists.
Personnel overhead-type expense, though it may overlap with employee benefits.
Classic SG&A component.
Balance-sheet asset line, not an income-statement SG&A expense.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A total row exists. Selected the strongest overhead buckets from the candidate list. Excluded depreciation/amortization and totals. Did not include 'Other personnel-related expenses' due to overlap risk with 'Employee benefits'.

candidates.why: Capitalized balance-sheet R&D asset, not a dedicated R&D expense line.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Balance-sheet contra-asset, not current-period R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Net - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing line, not an expense.

variables.notes: No dedicated income-statement R&D expense line is present. Balance-sheet R&D capitalization rows are not valid XRD expense inputs.

candidates.why: Broader finance cost line; useful fallback if specific interest expense is unavailable.
Specific interest expense subtype; valid but narrower than general interest expense.
Best direct match for interest expense.
Finance-related but not specifically interest.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental lease-interest disclosure; less preferred than primary statement lines.

variables.notes: Priority ordering follows the rule to prefer specific interest expense first, then narrower lease-interest, then broader financial expenses as fallback.

candidates.why: Income statement earnings line, not book equity.
Equity component only, not total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent
candidates.why: Best match because it excludes non-controlling interests.
Total equity but includes NCI in years where NCI exists; less preferred.
Balancing line, not a substantive equity measure.
Balancing line, not a substantive equity measure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing line, not equity.
Total balance sheet, not equity.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent

variables.notes: Chosen to exclude non-controlling interests and avoid double counting with MIB.

candidates.why: Income-statement attribution line, not balance-sheet minority interest stock.
Direct balance-sheet NCI line.
Equivalent balance-sheet label; useful fallback across years.
Income-statement attribution line, not balance-sheet minority interest stock.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income attribution, not balance-sheet minority interest.

variables.notes: Used balance-sheet NCI labels as priority list; these align with separate minority interest reporting while BE uses parent equity only.
